Name: AGameNetworkManager
Description: Handles game-specific networking management (cheat detection, bandwidth management, etc.).

Variables.Name: AdjustedNetSpeed
Variables.Description: Current adjusted net speed - Used for dynamically managing netspeed for listen servers

Variables.Name: LastNetSpeedUpdateTime
Variables.Description: Last time netspeed was updated for server (by client entering or leaving)

Variables.Name: TotalNetBandwidth
Variables.Description: Total available bandwidth for listen server, split dynamically across net connections

Variables.Name: MinDynamicBandwidth
Variables.Description: Minimum bandwidth dynamically set per connection

Variables.Name: MaxDynamicBandwidth
Variables.Description: Maximum bandwidth dynamically set per connection

Variables.Name: bIsStandbyCheckingEnabled
Variables.Description: Used to determine if checking for standby cheats should occur

Variables.Name: bHasStandbyCheatTriggered
Variables.Description: Used to determine whether we've already caught a cheat or not

Variables.Name: StandbyRxCheatTime
Variables.Description: The amount of time without packets before triggering the cheat code

Variables.Name: StandbyTxCheatTime
Variables.Description: The amount of time without packets before triggering the cheat code

Variables.Name: BadPingThreshold
Variables.Description: The point we determine the server is either delaying packets or has bad upstream

Variables.Name: PercentMissingForRxStandby
Variables.Description: The percentage of clients missing RX data before triggering the standby code

Variables.Name: PercentMissingForTxStandby
Variables.Description: The percentage of clients missing TX data before triggering the standby code

Variables.Name: PercentForBadPing
Variables.Description: The percentage of clients with bad ping before triggering the standby code

Variables.Name: JoinInProgressStandbyWaitTime
Variables.Description: The amount of time to wait before checking a connection for standby issues

Variables.Name: MoveRepSize
Variables.Description: Average size of replicated move packet (ServerMove() packet size) from player

Variables.Name: MAXPOSITIONERRORSQUARED
Variables.Description: MAXPOSITIONERRORSQUARED is the square of the max position error that is accepted (not corrected) in net play

Variables.Name: MAXNEARZEROVELOCITYSQUARED
Variables.Description: MAXNEARZEROVELOCITYSQUARED is the square of the max velocity that is considered zero (not corrected) in net play

Variables.Name: CLIENTADJUSTUPDATECOST
Variables.Description:
CLIENTADJUSTUPDATECOST is the bandwidth cost in bytes of sending a client adjustment update. 180 is greater than the actual cost, but represents a tweaked value reserving enough bandwidth for
	other updates sent to the client.  Increase this value to reduce client adjustment update frequency, or if the amount of data sent in the clientadjustment() call increases

Variables.Name: MAXCLIENTUPDATEINTERVAL
Variables.Description: MAXCLIENTUPDATEINTERVAL is the maximum time between movement updates from the client before the server forces an update.

Variables.Name: MaxMoveDeltaTime
Variables.Description: MaxMoveDeltaTime is the default maximum time delta of CharacterMovement ServerMoves. Should be less than or equal to MAXCLIENTUPDATEINTERVAL, otherwise server will interfere by forcing position updates.

Variables.Name: ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime
Variables.Description:
ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime is the default minimum time delta of CharacterMovement client moves to the server. When updates occur more frequently, they may be combined to save bandwidth.
	  This value is not used when player count is over ClientNetSendMoveThrottleOverPlayerCount or player net speed is <= ClientNetSendMoveThrottleAtNetSpeed (see ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeThrottled).

Variables.Name: ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeThrottled
Variables.Description: ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeThrottled is used in place of ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime when player count is high or net speed is low. See ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime for more info.

Variables.Name: ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeStationary
Variables.Description: ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeStationary is used when players are determined to not be moving or changing their view. See ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime for more info.

Variables.Name: ClientNetSendMoveThrottleAtNetSpeed
Variables.Description: When player net speed (CurrentNetSpeed, based on ConfiguredInternetSpeed or ConfiguredLanSpeed) is less than or equal to this amount, ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeThrottled is used instead of ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime.

Variables.Name: ClientNetSendMoveThrottleOverPlayerCount
Variables.Description: When player count is greater than this amount, ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTimeThrottled is used instead of ClientNetSendMoveDeltaTime.

Variables.Name: ClientAuthorativePosition
Variables.Description: If client update is within MAXPOSITIONERRORSQUARED then he is authorative on his final position

Variables.Name: ClientErrorUpdateRateLimit
Variables.Description: Minimum delay between the server sending error corrections to a client, in seconds.

Variables.Name: bMovementTimeDiscrepancyDetection
Variables.Description: Whether movement time discrepancy detection is enabled.

Variables.Name: bMovementTimeDiscrepancyResolution
Variables.Description: Whether movement time discrepancy resolution is enabled (when detected, make client movement "pay back" excessive time discrepancies)

Variables.Name: MovementTimeDiscrepancyMaxTimeMargin
Variables.Description: Maximum time client can be ahead before triggering movement time discrepancy detectionresolution (if enabled).

Variables.Name: MovementTimeDiscrepancyMinTimeMargin
Variables.Description: Maximum time client can be behind.

Variables.Name: MovementTimeDiscrepancyResolutionRate
Variables.Description:
During time discrepancy resolution, we "pay back" the time discrepancy at this rate for future moves until total error is zero.
	  1.0 = 100% resolution rate, meaning the next X ServerMoves from the client are fully paying back the time, 
	  0.5 = 50% resolution rate, meaning future ServerMoves will spend 50% of tick continuing to move the character and 50% paying back.
	  Lowering from 100% could be used to produce less severenoticeable corrections, although typically we would want to correct
	  the client as quickly as possible.

Variables.Name: MovementTimeDiscrepancyDriftAllowance
Variables.Description:
Accepted drift in clocks between client and server as a percent per second allowed. 
	 
	  0.0 is "no forgiveness" and all logic would run on raw values, no tampering on the server side.
	  0.02 would be a 2% per second difference "forgiven" - if the time discrepancy in a given second was less than 2%,
	  the error handlingdetection code effectively ignores it.
	  
	  Increasing this value above 0% lessens the chance of false positives on time discrepancy (burst packet loss, performance
	  hitches), but also means anyone tampering with their client time below that percent will not be detected and no resolution
	  action will be taken, and anyone above that threshold will still gain the advantage of this % of time boost (if running at 
	  10% speed-up and this value is 0.05 or 5% allowance, they would only be resolved down to a 5% speed boost).
	 
	  Time discrepancy detection code DOES keep track of LifetimeRawTimeDiscrepancy, which is unaffected by this drift allowance,
	  so cheating below DriftAllowance percent could be tracked and acted on outside of an individual game. For example, if DriftAllowance
	  was 0.05 (meaning we're not going to actively prevent any cheating below 5% boosts to ensure less false positives for normal players),
	  we could still post-process analytics of the game showing that Player X regularly runs at 4% speed boost and take action.

Variables.Name: bMovementTimeDiscrepancyForceCorrectionsDuringResolution
Variables.Description:
Whether client moves should be force corrected during time discrepancy resolution, useful for projects that have lenient 
	  move error toleranceClientAuthorativePosition enabled.

Variables.Name: bUseDistanceBasedRelevancy
Variables.Description: If true, actor network relevancy is constrained by whether they are within their NetCullDistanceSquared from the client's view point.
